name: ParaView
description: An open-source, multi-platform data analysis and 3D visualisation application for extremely large datasets. Built for scientific and engineering visualisation.
whyUseIt: ParaView can visualise datasets that are far too large for Matplotlib or MATLAB — billions of data points, 3D volumes, time-series of fluid simulations. It is the standard tool for post-processing computational physics results.
fieldPosition: Computational physics — 3D visualisation of simulation results (CFD, FEM, molecular dynamics)
physicistWorkflow: Load simulation output files, apply filters (slice, isosurface, streamline), adjust colour maps, and export images or animations.
supervisorExpects: Clear, informative 3D visualisations that communicate simulation results effectively.
computationalImpact: Makes it possible to explore and communicate results from large-scale 3D simulations.

vocabulary.definition: A processing step applied to data — slicing, contouring, integrating, etc.
The chain of filters applied from data source to visualisation

beginnerMistakes: Using inappropriate colour maps (e.g. rainbow) that hide features
Not setting the correct data range for the colour scale
